quote: The stock doesn't know you own it. You feel emotional about a stock, but the stock doesn't feel anything about you.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: In this business, if you're good, you're right six times out of ten. You're never going to be right nine times out of ten.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: Far more money has been lost by investors preparing for corrections, or trying to anticipate corrections, than has been lost in corrections themselves.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: Know what you own, and know why you own it.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: Go for a business that any idiot can run — because eventually, any idiot probably is going to run it.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: Long shots almost never pay off.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: You should be able to explain why you own a stock in such simple terms that a fifth grader could understand it.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: Numbers are nice, but the basic story line is what matters.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: Owning stocks is like having children — don't get involved with more than you can handle.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: The best stock to buy may be the one you already own.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: Investing without research is like playing stud poker and never looking at the cards.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: Time is on your side when you own shares of superior companies.
speaker: Peter Lynch
